seoTitle: Process Credit Card Payments in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to charge a customer's credit card directly through QuickBooks Online using Sales Receipts, invoices, or secure payment links.
intro: Chasing paper checks or juggling a separate card terminal slows down getting paid. QuickBooks Payments lets you charge a customer's card directly from a Sales Receipt, take details over the phone, or send a secure pay link attached to an invoice. Here's how the process works.

steps.heading: Start a Sales Receipt or Receive Payment
steps.body: Click + New, then choose Sales Receipt or Receive Payment under the Customers column.

steps.heading: Select the customer and payment method
steps.body: Choose the client from the dropdown, then set Credit Card as the payment method.

steps.heading: Enter card details securely
steps.body: Click Enter Card Details to open the encrypted Intuit input form, then type the card number, expiration date, and billing zip code.

steps.heading: Process the transaction
steps.body: Check the Process Credit Card box to instruct QuickBooks to run the real-time charge.

steps.heading: Or send a pay link on an invoice
steps.body: Alternatively, enable the credit card pay option on an emailed invoice so customers can enter their own card details through a secure self-serve link, then track the settlement in your Deposits ledger.

faq.question: Is it safe to type customer card numbers directly into QuickBooks?
faq.answer: Yes, the card entry form runs through Intuit's encrypted processing system rather than storing raw numbers in your regular records.

faq.question: What's the difference between charging a card directly and sending a payment link?
faq.answer: Charging directly means you key in the details yourself, while a payment link lets the customer securely enter their own card information.

faq.question: Where do processed payments show up afterward?
faq.answer: They flow into your Deposits ledger, where you can track settlement timelines and payout batches.

faq.question: Do I need a special account to process credit cards this way?
faq.answer: Yes, this relies on QuickBooks Payments being activated as your merchant services provider.
